seed_problem: Partition a weighted graph into two balanced halves maximizing cut weight; checker validates balance and scores cut/baseline.
mutation: composite: flip the objective from balanced MAX-cut to balanced MIN k-cut (balanced graph partitioning / the METIS objective) AND generalize from two halves to k balanced yards of exactly n/k hives; instances are planted as k index-uncorrelated communities with heavy intra-community drift plus light cross-community noise, so the checker's fixed index-block reference cut is mediocre while the optimal balanced partition leaves only the noise. Minimization makes the score ratio B/F reward genuine structure recovery, and greedy vs. swap local search vs. multi-restart diverge.
title: Apiary Yard Split: Minimum Cross-Yard Bee Drift
strategies: reference index-block split baseline: hives 1..n/k to yard 0, next n/k to yard 1, etc.
greedy constructive: place hives in a degree-sorted order, assigning each to the yard (with remaining capacity) to which it has the most drift already placed, so heavy drift stays intra-yard
balance-preserving swap local search: start from greedy and repeatedly swap two hives in different yards whenever the swap lowers total cross-yard drift (hill climbing with a move budget)
multi-restart / Kernighan-Lin style refinement to recover the planted balanced communities and approach the minimum k-cut
